title: The Iliad
author: Homer
summary: This edition presents Alexander Pope’s translation of Homer’s Iliad, with Theodore A. Buckley’s notes and Flaxman’s illustrations. The introductory essay surveys literary scepticism, critiques modern criticism, and debates Homeric authorship and biography. It reproduces a Herodotean life of Homer—Melesigenes—tracing origins in Cumæ and Smyrna, his adoption by Phemius, journeys with Mentes, and eventual poverty-driven wanderings through Phocœa, Chios, and Erythræ. The edition includes full contents (twenty-four books), maps, and numerous plates depicting gods, heroes, scenes, and funeral rites, framing the epic’s narrative of the Trojan War and its principal figures. It also reproduces Pope’s preface and explanatory notes for readers.
